title: Result<T, E>
lead: Result<T, E> is the explicit error-return track: an ADT enum with Ok(T) and Err(E).

sections.heading: ADT shape
sections.markdown: `Result<T, E>` is a prelude ADT enum: `Ok(T)` carries success, `Err(E)` carries failure. Prefer an ADT enum for `E` when the failure causes are enumerable, so `match` can check exhaustiveness.

sections.heading: Common methods
bullets: `unwrap()` extracts `Ok` or throws on `Err` when `E` is an `Exception` subtype

sections.heading: Bridge keywords
bullets: `try! result` early-returns `Err` inside a Result-returning function
`try! result` throws when crossing from Result into the exception track and `E` is an `Exception` subtype
`try? result` converts `Err` to `null` and discards the cause
`catch! expr` catches exceptions and converts them to `Result.Err`
